description: Mirror CI boot-lint and eval-probes gates into release.yml and gate the release (GHCR publish + GitHub Release) job on all three, closing the CI-parity gap. Harness-infra only (GitHub Actions workflow + rule doc + CHANGELOG).

userStories.title: Add boot-lint job to release.yml
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want the release pipeline to shellcheck the boot scripts and hadolint the Dockerfile it is about to build, so a lint regression cannot ship in a published image.
acceptanceCriteria: `.github/workflows/release.yml` defines a top-level job `boot-lint` whose steps match `.github/workflows/ci-harness.yml`'s `boot-lint` job: a 'Shellcheck boot scripts' step running `shellcheck -S warning .devcontainer/*.sh install/*.sh scripts/*.sh workspace/*.sh` (same shellcheck-install guard) and a 'Hadolint Dockerfile' step using the same SHA-pinned `hadolint/hadolint-action`, `dockerfile: .devcontainer/Dockerfile`, `config: .hadolint.yaml`, `failure-threshold: warning`.
The job runs on `ubuntu-latest` and checks out the repo (`actions/checkout@v4`).
The `boot-lint` job declares `permissions:` with `contents: read` (least-privilege; matches the `validate` job convention).

Structural check (no YAML parser ships in the sandbox): `grep -E '^  boot-lint:' .github/workflows/release.yml` matches; `grep -F 'hadolint/hadolint-action' .github/workflows/release.yml`, `grep -F '.hadolint.yaml' .github/workflows/release.yml`, and `grep -F 'failure-threshold: warning' .github/workflows/release.yml` all match.
No change to `.github/workflows/ci-harness.yml` (verify with `git diff --quiet -- .github/workflows/ci-harness.yml`).
Repo checks pass: `pnpm test:scripts` exits 0 and the pre-commit hook succeeds.
userStories.notes: Implemented boot-lint in release.yml with checkout, shellcheck guard, SHA-pinned hadolint action, .hadolint.yaml config, warning threshold, and contents: read permissions.

userStories.title: Add eval-probes job to release.yml
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want the release pipeline to run the eval probe regression gate, so a probe regression is caught before the image and Release ship.
acceptanceCriteria: `.github/workflows/release.yml` defines a top-level job `eval-probes` whose steps match `.github/workflows/ci-harness.yml`'s `eval-probes` job: checkout (`actions/checkout@v4`) then a single step running `bash .claude/skills/eval/run.sh`.
The `eval-probes` job runs on `ubuntu-latest` and declares `permissions:` with `contents: read`.
Structural check: `grep -E '^  eval-probes:' .github/workflows/release.yml` matches and `grep -F 'bash .claude/skills/eval/run.sh' .github/workflows/release.yml` matches.
No change to `.github/workflows/ci-harness.yml` and no change to `.claude/skills/eval/run.sh` (verify both with `git diff --quiet`).
Repo checks pass: `pnpm test:scripts` exits 0 and the pre-commit hook succeeds.
userStories.notes: Implemented eval-probes in release.yml with checkout, eval run.sh invocation, and contents: read permissions.

userStories.title: Gate the release job on all three gate jobs
userStories.description: As a release engineer, I want the GHCR publish + GitHub Release step blocked unless validate, boot-lint, and eval-probes all pass, so the publish never proceeds on a partially-green pipeline.
acceptanceCriteria: The `release` job's `needs:` lists exactly `validate`, `boot-lint`, and `eval-probes` (order not significant). Verify: `grep -E '^    needs:' .github/workflows/release.yml` shows all three names.
The `validate` job's steps are unchanged (only its presence as a `needs:` target matters; do not edit its steps).
Every job named in any `needs:` is defined as a top-level job in the file (no dangling dependency): `validate`, `boot-lint`, `eval-probes` each appear as a `^  <name>:` job key.
Repo checks pass: `pnpm test:scripts` exits 0 and the pre-commit hook succeeds.
userStories.notes: release job now needs [validate, boot-lint, eval-probes]; all referenced jobs are defined.

userStories.title: Update git.md rule and CHANGELOG
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want the release documentation to accurately describe the validate gate and the change recorded in the changelog.
acceptanceCriteria: The sentence in `context/rules/git.md` that says the validate job runs 'lint, format check, build, test, and the root-scripts tests (mirroring CI)' is updated via a TARGETED edit (no whole-file rewrite) to enumerate: lint, format check, build, test, root-scripts tests, boot-path lint (shellcheck + hadolint), and eval-probe regression — still ending '(mirroring CI)'.

`context/rules/git.md` is on `.claude/protected-paths.txt` (as the `.claude/rules/git.md` symlink); this is a documentation EDIT, not a deletion, so it does not violate the protected-path gate. `git diff -- context/rules/git.md` shows only the one sentence changed.
`CHANGELOG.md` has a new bullet under `## [Unreleased]` > `### Changed` describing the release-gate parity change and linking `#111`.
No other behavioral content in `git.md` is altered.
Repo checks pass: `pnpm test:scripts` exits 0 and the pre-commit hook succeeds.
userStories.notes: Updated context/rules/git.md release gate sentence and CHANGELOG [Unreleased] Changed entry for #111.
